items.title: Rolling out crash monitoring at scale
items.summary: End-to-end initiative to instrument, deploy, and drive adoption of crash monitoring across a mobile app serving millions of active users.

sections.title: Context
sections.content: The app had no centralized crash visibility. Engineers were flying blind post-release, relying on user reports to catch regressions.

sections.title: Approach
sections.content: Designed the SDK integration strategy, wrote internal adoption docs, coordinated with platform and observability teams, and ran the rollout in phases to minimize blast radius.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.content: Crash monitoring live across 100% of Android surface area. Mean time to detection dropped significantly. Became the canonical reference implementation for future observability tooling.

items.title: Kotlin Multiplatform module migration
items.summary: Led migration of several business-logic modules from Android-specific Kotlin to KMP, enabling code sharing with the iOS team.

sections.title: Challenge
sections.content: Modules had Android-specific dependencies baked in, test coverage was spotty, and the iOS team had a parallel implementation with drift.

sections.title: Execution
sections.content: Prioritized modules by blast radius and coupling. Rewrote one module at a time using expect/actual, added shared unit tests, and documented each migration as a repeatable pattern.

sections.title: Result
sections.content: Several core modules now shared across platforms. Reduced code duplication and eliminated sync drift between Android and iOS implementations.
